title: KnowsRecord
description: A minimal, verifiable companion sidecar record for research artifacts. Binds author-asserted statements, evidence, provenance, and optional action hooks to an existing human artifact in a form agents can consume directly.

$schema.description: JSON Schema URI for this record.

knows_version.description: Knows spec version this record conforms to (semver).

record_id.description: Globally unique identifier for this sidecar record. Recommended format: DOI-based or URL-based.

profile.description: Domain-specific extension profile and version. E.g., 'paper@1', 'repo@1', 'dataset@1', 'model@1', 'benchmark@1'.

subject_ref.description: Local ID referencing the primary artifact this record describes. MUST reference an id from the artifacts array. JSON Schema cannot enforce this cross-reference; use knows-lint for validation.

title.description: Human-readable title for this sidecar record.

summary.description: Brief description of what this sidecar covers.

coverage.description: Declares the scope of this record's coverage.

license.description: SPDX license identifier for this sidecar record. E.g., 'CC-BY-4.0'.

artifacts.description: The things being described: paper, repo, dataset, model, benchmark, cited work. Present even if empty.

statements.description: Claims, assumptions, limitations, methods, questions, definitions. Present even if empty.

evidence.description: Table cells, figures, experiment results, citation-backed support. Present even if empty.

relations.description: Typed edges across artifacts, statements, and evidence. Present even if empty.

abstract.description: Full abstract of the paper. Agents use this for L0 discovery (decide whether to read further). Complements 'summary' which describes the sidecar; 'abstract' describes the paper itself.

venue.description: Publication venue. E.g., 'CVPR 2016', 'Nature', 'arXiv preprint', 'ACM CCS 2016'. Agents use this to assess credibility and domain. Common values for non-published work: 'arXiv preprint', 'under review', 'submitted to [venue]', 'in preparation', 'technical report'.

venue_type.description: Publication status. Agents use this to calibrate trust: 'published' > 'preprint' > 'under_review' > 'in_preparation'.

year.description: Publication year of the primary artifact. Distinct from freshness.as_of (sidecar creation time).

language.description: Primary language of the artifact. ISO 639-1 code. Defaults to 'en' if omitted. Agents use this for multilingual filtering.

access.description: Access level of the primary artifact. Distinct from license: a CC-BY paper may still be behind a paywall. Agents use this to determine if they can retrieve the full text.

record_status.description: Lifecycle status of THIS SIDECAR RECORD. 'retracted' = the described artifact has been retracted. 'superseded' = a newer sidecar version exists. Agents should check this before trusting the record. Distinct from Statement.status which is per-claim.

replaces.description: record_id of the previous version this record supersedes. Forms a singly-linked version chain: agents follow replaces pointers to traverse history. The referenced record SHOULD set record_status to 'superseded'.

keywords.description: Search keywords for agent discovery. Include: domain terms, method names, dataset names, task names. Agents use these for L0 (discovery) tasks. Aim for 5-15 terms covering topic, method, and application.

authors.description: Authors of the primary artifact. Includes name, affiliation, and optional identifiers (ORCID, email, homepage).

funding.description: Funding sources. Agents use this for conflict-of-interest assessment and grant tracking.

resources.description: Computational, human, and material resources used. Critical for reproducibility assessment.

actions.description: Optional machine-invokable affordances. A valid record may have zero actions.

provenance.description: Who/what produced this sidecar record. Serves as DEFAULT provenance for all statements and evidence that do not specify their own. Per-item provenance overrides this when present.

extensions.description: Domain-specific extension data. Structure depends on profile.

statements.description: How complete. exhaustive='every claim in the paper', main_claims_only='top-level claims', key_claims_and_limitations='claims + known limitations', partial='selected subset'.

evidence.description: How complete. exhaustive='all evidence', key_evidence_only='main tables/figures', partial='selected subset'.

x_extensions.description: Extension point for forward-compatible custom fields.

Artifact.description: A research artifact being described. Must have at least one of identifiers, locators, or representations to be discoverable. Cited external works (role='cited') may have only identifiers.

artifact_type.description: Type of research artifact. paper='journal/conference paper', repository='code repo', dataset='training/eval data', model='ML model or pretrained weights', benchmark='evaluation suite', software='tool or library'.

role.description: Role relative to this sidecar. subject='the main artifact described', supporting='used by the main artifact', cited='referenced but external'.

representations.description: Concrete surfaces of this artifact (HTML, PDF, file, API). Can be empty for cited external works.

external_metadata_refs.description: Pointers to existing metadata standards (CodeMeta, CITATION.cff, Croissant, model card).

x_extensions.description: Extension point for Artifact-level custom fields. Forward-compatible: new fields can be added here without breaking schema validation.
Extension point for forward-compatible custom fields.

version.description: Git commit hash, tag, or version identifier.

x_extensions.description: Extension point for forward-compatible custom fields.
Extension point for forward-compatible custom fields.

standard.description: Which metadata standard this points to.

x_extensions.description: Extension point for forward-compatible custom fields.

statement_type.description: What kind of statement. E.g., claim='Our method achieves SOTA', assumption='We assume IID data', limitation='Only tested on English', method='We use SGD with momentum', question='Can this scale to 1B params?', definition='We define X as...'.

modality.description: How the statement is grounded. empirical='backed by experiments/data', theoretical='derived from theory/proof', descriptive='states a fact without judgment', normative='prescribes what should be done or is good/bad'.

text.description: The statement content in natural language.

about_ref.description: Local ID of the artifact this statement is about.

status.description: Current lifecycle status. asserted='active claim', retracted='withdrawn by authors', superseded='replaced by newer version', under_review='not yet finalized'.

source_anchors.description: Where in the human artifact this statement appears.

confidence.description: Two-dimensional confidence: epistemic (science strength) x encoding (sidecar fidelity).

citation_refs.description: Local IDs of cited artifacts relevant to this statement.

x_extensions.description: Extension point for Statement-level custom fields. Forward-compatible: new fields can be added here without breaking schema validation.

evidence_type.description: What kind of evidence. table_result='data from a table', figure='chart or diagram', experiment_run='executed experiment', proof='formal mathematical proof', case_study='detailed case analysis', clinical_trial='medical trial', survey_result='questionnaire/interview data', qualitative_analysis='thematic/content analysis', observation='direct observation', simulation='computational simulation'.

source_anchors.description: Where in the human artifact this evidence appears.

observations.description: Structured numeric measurements extracted from the evidence.

x_extensions.description: Extension point for Evidence-level custom fields. Forward-compatible: new fields can be added here without breaking schema validation.

Observation.description: A structured measurement or qualitative finding. Use 'value' for numeric data (e.g., accuracy=0.95) or 'qualitative_value' for non-numeric findings (e.g., crystal_structure='hexagonal', theme='participant autonomy'). At least one of value or qualitative_value should be present.

metric.description: Name of the metric or observation dimension. E.g., 'accuracy', 'crystal_structure', 'theme', 'ruling', 'p_value'.

value.description: Numeric value. Use for quantitative observations.

qualitative_value.description: Non-numeric value. Use for qualitative observations (e.g., 'hexagonal', 'double helix', 'statistically significant', 'guilty'). For disciplines where evidence is not numeric.

error.description: Standard deviation or confidence interval half-width.

n_runs.description: Number of runs used to compute this measurement.

qualifiers.description: Additional context: benchmark_ref, split, compared_to_ref, compared_to_value, seed, etc.

subject_ref.description: Local ID of the subject entity.

predicate.description: Relationship type. supported_by='claim backed by evidence', challenged_by='claim questioned by evidence/review', depends_on='claim requires this assumption', limited_by='claim constrained by this limitation', cites='references another work', uses='employs a tool/dataset', evaluates_on='tested on a benchmark'.

object_ref.description: Local ID of the object entity. For cross-record references, use 'record_id#local_id'.

citation_intent.description: Why cited (only with predicate='cites'). supports='cited work backs this claim', extends='builds on cited work', uses_method='borrows technique from cited work', compares_to='benchmarked against cited work', contradicts='disagrees with cited work'.

x_extensions.description: Extension point for Relation-level custom fields. Forward-compatible: new fields can be added here without breaking schema validation.

target_ref.description: Local ID of the artifact this action operates on.

safety.description: Safety constraints for this action. MANDATORY when action is present.

x_extensions.description: Extension point for Action-level custom fields. Forward-compatible: new fields can be added here without breaking schema validation.

input_schema.description: JSON Schema describing the input parameters.

output_schema.description: JSON Schema describing the expected output.

network.description: Whether the action requires network access.

secrets_required.description: Whether API keys or credentials are needed.

x_extensions.description: Extension point for forward-compatible custom fields.

representation_ref.description: Local ID of the representation this anchor points into.

locator_type.description: How to find content. page_range='e.g., pages 3-5', section='e.g., Section 3.1', table='e.g., Table 2', figure='e.g., Figure 1', paragraph='specific paragraph', line_range='e.g., lines 45-60'.

quote.description: Optional verbatim quote from the source for verification.

x_extensions.description: Extension point for forward-compatible custom fields.

derived_from.description: Local IDs or anchors this was derived from.

verification.description: Automated verification checks that have been run.

x_extensions.description: Extension point for forward-compatible custom fields.

version.description: Version of the tool (if type is 'tool').

x_extensions.description: Extension point for forward-compatible custom fields.

Author.description: An author of the research artifact. Provides structured bio/affiliation for agent discovery and attribution. For double-blind submissions, set anonymous: true and omit identifying fields.

name.description: Full name. Use 'Anonymous' for double-blind, 'Independent Researcher' for unaffiliated authors.

anonymous.description: If true, author identity is withheld (double-blind review). Name may be 'Anonymous' or a pseudonym.

affiliation.description: Primary institutional affiliation. Use 'Independent' for independent researchers, omit for anonymous.

email.description: Contact email (optional, may be omitted for privacy).

role.description: Role in the authorship. Helps agents identify who to contact or attribute.

x_extensions.description: Extension point for forward-compatible custom fields.

by.description: Tool or person that performed the check.

details.description: Additional details about the check result.

x_extensions.description: Extension point for forward-compatible custom fields.

FundingSource.description: A funding source for the research. Enables conflict-of-interest assessment and grant outcome tracking.

x_extensions.description: Extension point for forward-compatible custom fields.

Resources.description: Computational, human, and material resources used. Critical for reproducibility: another researcher must know what is needed to replicate the work.

participants.description: Number of study participants (for user studies, clinical trials).

facilities.description: Physical facilities. E.g., 'wet lab', 'wind tunnel', 'synchrotron beamline', 'clean room'.

estimated_cost.description: Estimated total cost of the research. E.g., '$5,000 compute + $2,000 annotation'.

Confidence.description: Two-dimensional confidence. claim_strength = how strong is the science? extraction_fidelity = how accurately does this sidecar capture the source?

claim_strength.description: How strong the underlying scientific statement is. High = well-supported by evidence, Medium = some support, Low = speculative or preliminary.

extraction_fidelity.description: How faithfully this sidecar field captures the original source. High = verbatim or very close, Medium = summarized, Low = loosely paraphrased.

x_extensions.description: Extension point for forward-compatible custom fields.

source.description: Version of the human artifact this describes (git commit, DOI version, etc.).

x_extensions.description: Extension point for forward-compatible custom fields.

as_of.description: When this record was last verified as accurate.

update_policy.description: How this record is expected to change over time.

review_after.description: Suggested date to re-verify this record.

stale_after.description: After this date, the record should be considered potentially stale.

x_extensions.description: Extension point for forward-compatible custom fields.
